name: Ribs with a Peach Barbecue Sauce
recipeIngredient: 5 Pounds Pork Or Lamb Spareribs, meaty
1/4 Cup Mirin Or Medium Dry Sherry
1 1/2 Cups Pureed Peach Pulp (4 Large Peaches)
1 Tablespoon Light Brown Sugar (Packed), or more to taste
1 Teaspoon Chinese Chile Paste With Garlic
Salt And Freshly Ground Black Pepper -- to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the ribs into individual portions and set aside in a large bowl.
2. Make the barbecue sauce: Heat the oil in a saucepan over low heat. Add the garlic and ginger and stir until aromatic, about 30 seconds.
3. Add the mirin and reduce over high heat for about 2 minutes. Add the remaining sauce ingredients and simmer for about 20 minutes, stirring.
4. Arrange the spareribs in a large dish. Brush with generous amounts of the sauce and refrigerate for 12 hours or overnight.
5. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Arrange the spareribs in a shallow baking pan and bake, brushing frequently with the remaining sauce, until the ribs are well browned, crisp, and cooked through, about 1 1/4 hours.
6. Serve at once.
Yield: 4-6 servings
